Dominik Livakovic.bio: Croatia's first-choice goalkeeper who became a national hero at the 2022 World Cup with his penalty-saving heroics. Livakovic moved to Fenerbahce in Turkey after starring for Dinamo Zagreb.
Dominik Livakovic.playingStyle: Athletic goalkeeper with outstanding penalty-saving ability, excellent reflexes, and confidence in big moments
Dominik Livakovic.worldCup2026Role: Undisputed starting goalkeeper and key figure in tournament knockout scenarios
Dominik Livakovic.notableFact: Saved three penalties in the shootout against Japan at the 2022 World Cup, becoming only the fourth goalkeeper to achieve this in World Cup history

Josip Stanisic.bio: A Bayern Munich product born in Germany to Croatian parents, Stanisic chose to represent Croatia internationally. He is a modern, athletic full-back with experience at the highest level of European football.
Josip Stanisic.playingStyle: Athletic, overlapping right-back with strong defensive instincts and composure on the ball from his Bayern Munich training
Josip Stanisic.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back bringing top-level European experience to the defense
Josip Stanisic.notableFact: Was born and raised in Munich, Germany, and came through Bayern Munich's academy before choosing to represent Croatia

Borna Barisic.bio: A left-back who became a fan favorite at Rangers in Scotland, known for his delivery from set pieces and wide areas. Barisic returned to Croatian football with NK Osijek after his time in Glasgow.
Borna Barisic.playingStyle: Attacking left-back with exceptional set-piece delivery and accurate crossing from deep positions
Borna Barisic.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup left-back and set-piece specialist option
Borna Barisic.notableFact: Was a key part of the Rangers side that reached the 2022 Europa League final, providing crucial assists throughout the campaign

Ivan Perisic.bio: One of Croatia's most decorated players with over 135 caps, Perisic has won the Champions League with Inter Milan and played in the 2018 World Cup final. A tireless, versatile attacker across multiple top leagues.
Ivan Perisic.playingStyle: Powerful, two-footed winger who combines pace with physicality, dangerous in the air for a wide player, and a relentless work rate
Ivan Perisic.worldCup2026Role: Veteran squad member providing experience and tactical flexibility across the forward line
Ivan Perisic.notableFact: Scored in the 2018 World Cup final against France and has scored at three different World Cups for Croatia

Martin Erlic.bio: A Croatian-born center-back who has developed his career in Italy and Denmark. Erlic is a physically imposing defender who provides solid depth to Croatia's backline options.
Martin Erlic.playingStyle: Strong, physical center-back who excels in aerial duels and is comfortable defending in a low block
Martin Erlic.worldCup2026Role: Defensive depth option as a backup center-back in the squad
Martin Erlic.notableFact: Spent formative years in Italian football at Sassuolo and Spezia, developing his game in one of Europe's most tactically demanding leagues

Dejan Lovren.bio: A World Cup finalist in 2018 and Champions League winner with Liverpool in 2019, Lovren is one of Croatia's most experienced defenders. Now at PAOK in Greece, he brings invaluable tournament experience.
Dejan Lovren.playingStyle: Aggressive, commanding center-back with leadership qualities, strong in the air and physical in duels
Dejan Lovren.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive leader providing tournament experience and mentorship to younger defenders
Dejan Lovren.notableFact: Won the Champions League with Liverpool in 2019 and played every minute of Croatia's run to the 2018 World Cup final

Lovro Majer.bio: A technically gifted Croatian playmaker who impressed at Rennes before moving to Wolfsburg in the Bundesliga. Majer is seen as one of the heirs to Croatia's rich midfield tradition.
Lovro Majer.playingStyle: Creative, technically excellent playmaker who picks out passes in tight spaces, with a sweet left foot and eye for goal
Lovro Majer.worldCup2026Role: Key creative midfielder and potential successor to the aging playmaker role in Croatia's system
Lovro Majer.notableFact: Won the Croatian Football Federation's Best Young Player award and is widely regarded as the next great Croatian midfielder

Mateo Kovacic.bio: A silky midfielder who has won the Champions League with Real Madrid and Chelsea, and now anchors Manchester City's midfield. Kovacic is the epitome of the Croatian midfield tradition of technical excellence.
Mateo Kovacic.playingStyle: Elegant, press-resistant midfielder who glides past opponents with the ball and maintains possession under intense pressure
Mateo Kovacic.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder forming the engine room alongside Modric and Brozovic
Mateo Kovacic.notableFact: One of only a handful of players to have won the Champions League with three different clubs (Real Madrid could have been followed by Chelsea)

Andrej Kramaric.bio: Hoffenheim's all-time top scorer, Kramaric has been one of the Bundesliga's most consistent strikers for nearly a decade. He is a clinical finisher with an excellent international record for Croatia.
Andrej Kramaric.playingStyle: Intelligent striker who drops deep to link play, combines well with midfielders, and is deadly in and around the penalty box
Andrej Kramaric.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking option as a starter or impactful substitute bringing goalscoring pedigree
Andrej Kramaric.notableFact: Holds the Bundesliga record for most goals scored by a Croatian player and is Hoffenheim's all-time leading scorer

Luka Modric.bio: A Ballon d'Or winner and one of the greatest midfielders in football history, Modric led Croatia to the 2018 World Cup final and the 2022 third-place finish. Now at AC Milan after a legendary spell at Real Madrid.
Luka Modric.playingStyle: Sublime technical maestro with unmatched vision, passing range, and ability to control the tempo of any match despite his diminutive frame
Luka Modric.worldCup2026Role: Captain and midfield conductor, the heartbeat of everything Croatia do, likely in his farewell World Cup
Luka Modric.notableFact: Won the 2018 Ballon d'Or, the Golden Ball at the 2018 World Cup, and six Champions League titles with Real Madrid

Marcelo Brozovic.bio: The metronome of Croatia's midfield, Brozovic was the highest-distance runner at the 2018 World Cup and has been the team's indispensable deep-lying playmaker. He moved to Al-Nassr in Saudi Arabia after a stellar Inter Milan career.
Marcelo Brozovic.playingStyle: Deep-lying playmaker who covers enormous ground, distributes with precision, and dictates play from the base of midfield
Marcelo Brozovic.worldCup2026Role: Starting defensive midfielder and metronome who sets the rhythm for the entire team
Marcelo Brozovic.notableFact: Covered more distance than any other player at the 2018 World Cup, running over 72km across seven matches

Ivica Ivusic.bio: A capable Croatian goalkeeper who has gained experience in domestic and Cypriot football. Ivusic provides reliable depth in the goalkeeping department behind Livakovic.
Ivica Ivusic.playingStyle: Solid, reliable goalkeeper with good shot-stopping and command of his penalty area
Ivica Ivusic.notableFact: Made several crucial saves during Croatia's qualifying campaign, proving himself as a dependable backup

Mario Pasalic.bio: A versatile attacking midfielder who found his best football at Atalanta in Serie A after numerous loan spells from Chelsea. Pasalic has become a consistent goal contributor from midfield.
Mario Pasalic.playingStyle: Goal-scoring midfielder who times his runs into the box perfectly, combining aerial ability with technical skill in the final third
Mario Pasalic.worldCup2026Role: Versatile attacking midfield option who provides goal threat from the second line
Mario Pasalic.notableFact: Was on Chelsea's books for seven years but played for six different clubs on loan before Atalanta made his move permanent

Marko Livaja.bio: A passionate striker who became a hero at Hajduk Split after returning to Croatian football. Livaja's goalscoring exploits in the domestic league earned him regular international recognition.
Marko Livaja.playingStyle: Technically gifted striker with flair, creativity, and the ability to score spectacular goals from distance
Marko Livaja.worldCup2026Role: Attacking option off the bench bringing creativity and unpredictability
Marko Livaja.notableFact: Became a cult hero at Hajduk Split, where his passionate celebrations and spectacular goals made him an icon of Croatian domestic football

Ante Budimir.bio: A towering target man who has built a solid career in Spanish football with Osasuna. Budimir offers a different dimension to Croatia's attack with his aerial presence and hold-up play.
Ante Budimir.playingStyle: Classic target man who excels in the air, provides hold-up play, and brings teammates into the game in advanced areas
Ante Budimir.worldCup2026Role: Squad option as an aerial target man, particularly useful as a late-game plan B
Ante Budimir.notableFact: Has scored more La Liga goals than any other Croatian player in recent history during his time with Osasuna

Bruno Petkovic.bio: A key player for Dinamo Zagreb and Croatia, Petkovic has been crucial in major tournament moments including a dramatic late equalizer against Brazil at the 2022 World Cup.
Bruno Petkovic.playingStyle: Strong, intelligent striker with good technique, excellent hold-up play, and the ability to score crucial goals in big moments
Bruno Petkovic.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking option who thrives in big moments, a proven tournament performer
Bruno Petkovic.notableFact: Scored the 117th-minute equalizer against Brazil in the 2022 World Cup quarterfinals, one of the most dramatic goals in World Cup history

Luka Sucic.bio: An exciting young Croatian playmaker who plays for Real Sociedad in La Liga. Born in Austria to Croatian parents, Sucic is considered one of the brightest young talents in Croatian football.
Luka Sucic.playingStyle: Creative left-footed playmaker with excellent technique, composure beyond his years, and a dangerous long-range shot
Luka Sucic.worldCup2026Role: Emerging attacking midfielder seen as a long-term successor to Modric's creative influence
Luka Sucic.notableFact: Born in Linz, Austria, and chose to represent Croatia over Austria, following the path of several dual-national Croatian talents

Mislav Orsic.bio: A dynamic winger who became famous for his extraordinary hat-trick against Tottenham in the Europa League with Dinamo Zagreb. Orsic has been a consistent performer for club and country.
Mislav Orsic.playingStyle: Direct, pacy winger who cuts inside from the left with his stronger right foot, capable of scoring spectacular individual goals
Mislav Orsic.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide attacker providing depth and impact from the substitutes' bench
Mislav Orsic.notableFact: Scored a stunning hat-trick against Tottenham in the 2021 Europa League to knock them out, with the third goal being a 40-yard solo run

Borna Sosa.bio: A modern attacking left-back who made his name at VfB Stuttgart in the Bundesliga before moving to the Premier League with Crystal Palace. Sosa is prized for his dangerous crossing ability.
Borna Sosa.playingStyle: Attacking left-back with one of the best cross deliveries in European football, creating numerous chances from wide positions
Borna Sosa.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back providing width, crossing threat, and attacking impetus down the left flank
Borna Sosa.notableFact: Led the Bundesliga in crosses and was one of the top assist providers from a defensive position during his time at Stuttgart

Josip Sutalo.bio: A young Croatian center-back who moved to Ajax from Dinamo Zagreb, Sutalo has quickly adapted to football at the highest level and is seen as a key part of Croatia's defensive future.
Josip Sutalo.playingStyle: Athletic, ball-playing center-back with good recovery pace and composure in possession
Josip Sutalo.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back candidate representing the new generation of Croatian defenders
Josip Sutalo.notableFact: Was one of the youngest captains in Dinamo Zagreb history before earning his big move to Ajax

Igor Matanovic.bio: A towering young striker of Croatian descent who was born in Germany and plays for Eintracht Frankfurt in the Bundesliga. Matanovic represents the exciting future of Croatian attacking talent.
Igor Matanovic.playingStyle: Tall, powerful young striker with good technical ability for his size, aerial dominance, and improving finishing
Igor Matanovic.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking prospect providing squad depth and a different physical dimension up front
Igor Matanovic.notableFact: At 196cm, he is one of the tallest strikers in the Croatian setup, born and raised in Frankfurt, Germany

Josip Juranovic.bio: A versatile Croatian full-back who starred at Celtic before moving to Union Berlin in the Bundesliga. Juranovic was one of Croatia's standout performers at the 2022 World Cup.
Josip Juranovic.playingStyle: Tenacious, attack-minded full-back who defends aggressively, takes penalties, and provides consistent energy on the right side
Josip Juranovic.worldCup2026Role: Versatile full-back providing competition and rotation on the right side of defense
Josip Juranovic.notableFact: Took and scored penalties for Croatia at the 2022 World Cup despite being a full-back, showcasing his ice-cold composure

Duje Caleta-Car.bio: A physical center-back who developed in France with Marseille before moving to Real Sociedad in La Liga. Caleta-Car is a rugged, experienced defender with strong aerial qualities.
Duje Caleta-Car.playingStyle: Physical, aerial-dominant center-back who excels in defensive duels and provides a commanding presence at set pieces
Duje Caleta-Car.worldCup2026Role: Experienced center-back providing depth and aerial presence in the defensive rotation
Duje Caleta-Car.notableFact: Was reportedly close to signing for Liverpool before the move collapsed on deadline day, one of football's famous near-misses

Josko Gvardiol.bio: One of the most valuable defenders in world football, Gvardiol joined Manchester City for a record fee for a defender. The young Croatian is already regarded as one of the best center-backs on the planet.
Josko Gvardiol.playingStyle: Modern, ball-carrying center-back with explosive pace, power, and the ability to drive forward from the backline like a midfielder
Josko Gvardiol.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and defensive cornerstone, potentially Croatia's most important player alongside Modric
Josko Gvardiol.notableFact: Transferred to Manchester City for approximately 90 million euros, making him the most expensive defender in football history at the time

Dominik Kotarski.bio: A promising young Croatian goalkeeper who plays for FC Copenhagen in Denmark. Kotarski has shown impressive development and is being groomed as a future national team goalkeeper.
Dominik Kotarski.playingStyle: Young, agile goalkeeper with good reflexes and developing command of his area
Dominik Kotarski.worldCup2026Role: Young backup goalkeeper gaining tournament experience as part of the squad
Dominik Kotarski.notableFact: Has represented Croatia at various youth levels and is considered the long-term successor in the goalkeeping position

Nikola Vlasic.bio: A creative midfielder who plays for Torino in Serie A, Vlasic previously had spells at Everton, CSKA Moscow (where he won league Player of the Year), and West Ham. He is the son of Olympic champion Blanka Vlasic's brother.
Nikola Vlasic.playingStyle: Creative, technically skilled attacking midfielder with good dribbling, vision, and the ability to unlock defenses in the final third
Nikola Vlasic.worldCup2026Role: Creative attacking midfield option providing depth and quality in the final third
Nikola Vlasic.notableFact: His sister Blanka Vlasic is an Olympic high-jump medalist, making them one of Croatia's most famous sporting families
